commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an npm target availability preflight to `openclaw update` planning/apply paths and expands update CLI and npm registry spec tests for missing public npm targets, registry config handling, Bun skips, package-spec overrides, and semver-range parsing.

PR surface: Source +279, Tests +511. Total +790 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main resolves exact semver tags and prints dry-run output before the package install gate that would hit npm ETARGET. The original live `2026.5.26` window has passed, so regression proof should use a mocked absent target or a currently absent exact version.

target.title: Reject unpublished npm targets consistently in update dry-run
target.body:
Fixes #87320

## User-facing bug
`openclaw update --tag <version> --dry-run` could report a clean update plan when the requested OpenClaw version existed as a GitHub release but had not yet propagated to the npm registry. Running the same update without `--dry-run` then failed with npm `ETARGET`, so dry-run and apply gave contradictory signals during the npm propagation window.

## Exact repro
Source-level reproduction:

1. Use a package-manager install of OpenClaw.
2. Request an exact registry version target that is syntactically valid but absent from npm, for example:
   - `openclaw update --tag 2026.5.31 --dry-run --json`
3. Before this fix, the exact semver tag was accepted locally as `targetVersion: "2026.5.31"` without probing npm availability, so dry-run emitted a green plan.
4. Running apply with the same target later reached `npm install openclaw@2026.5.31` and failed with `ETARGET`/`E404`.
5. After this fix, dry-run and apply both fail early with a clear npm propagation message when the registry returns `HTTP 404` for the target.

## Fix summary
- Added an npm target availability preflight for package update targets that can be resolved through the npm registry.
- The preflight fails only when npm clearly reports the target as absent (`HTTP 404`), preserving existing behavior for transient network errors and non-registry package specs.
- The same preflight runs before both dry-run preview output and real package update install/stop-service work, so dry-run no longer green-lights a target apply will reject.
- The preflight now validates the actual resolved install spec, normalizes `v`-prefixed exact versions for registry lookup, and skips public-npm availability checks when `OPENCLAW_UPDATE_PACKAGE_SPEC` is explicitly authoritative.
- Registry probing now follows the actual resolved package manager path: npm global config for npm, pnpm config for pnpm, and no public npm availability preflight for Bun-managed installs.
- Public npm registry aliases are normalized, including HTTP and protocol-relative npmjs registry URLs, while custom registries keep Node runtime preflight coverage without forcing public npm target availability.

## Targeted tests
- Added dry-run coverage proving exact npm version targets absent from npm fail before JSON preview output.
- Added apply coverage proving the same absent target fails before `npm install` or managed service stop.
- Added coverage for `v`-prefixed exact versions and both tarball-style and registry-style package spec overrides.
- Added coverage for resolved global package managers, npm and pnpm registry config discovery, public npm registry aliases, custom registries, Bun-managed package updates, Node runtime preflight preservation, and semver-range rejection in registry npm specs.
- Re-ran focused update CLI and npm registry spec tests to catch package update regressions.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw update --tag <exact-version> --dry-run` and real apply now use the same npm target availability preflight, so dry-run does not approve a version npm will reject with `ETARGET`.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/update-dry-run-npm-target`, commit `ad2f05b3`, rebased onto `origin/main` at `be127318`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `npm view openclaw@2026.5.31 version --json`
- `env OPENCLAW_NO_RESPAWN=1 node --import tsx -e "import { fetchNpmPackageTargetStatus } from './src/infra/update-check.ts'; const status = await fetchNpmPackageTargetStatus({ target: '2026.5.31', timeoutMs: 5000 }); console.log(JSON.stringify(status));"`
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/update-cli.test.ts src/infra/npm-registry-spec.test.ts -t "Bun-managed package updates|resolved global manager|pnpm registry config|protocol-relative npmjs registry|HTTP npmjs registry|explicit npm version target|v-prefixed exact npm targets|digit-starting npm dist-tags|custom registry|Node runtime preflight|custom global registry|lowercase npm registry env precedence|npm userconfig|semver range package specs|package spec overrides|accepts|rejects"`
- `git diff --check origin/main`
- `codex review --base origin/main`

Evidence after fix:
- Real npm registry lookup failed as expected after the latest rebase: `npm error code E404`, `No match found for version 2026.5.31`, and `'openclaw@2026.5.31' is not in this registry.`
- The OpenClaw source npm target lookup returned `{"target":"2026.5.31","version":null,"nodeEngine":null,"error":"HTTP 404"}` after the latest rebase.
- Focused Vitest passed after the latest rebase and review fixes: `Test Files 2 passed (2)`, `Tests 36 passed | 163 skipped (199)`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `codex review --base origin/main` reported no actionable correctness issues in the changed update preflight or npm spec validation paths.

Observed result after fix: Exact npm targets absent from the public npm registry fail before both JSON dry-run preview output and real package install/service stop work; explicit package spec overrides and Bun-managed installs keep their authoritative behavior.

What was not tested: A full source-entry `node --import tsx src/entry.ts update --channel stable --tag 2026.5.31 --dry-run --json --timeout 5` proof was attempted with isolated `OPENCLAW_PROFILE`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, but the local source-entry update process hung before emitting output and had to be stopped. Because that output is not conclusive, it is intentionally not used as pass evidence.

## Not tested / known gaps
- No live package-manager update was run against a real globally installed OpenClaw package; coverage is through a real npm registry lookup, the real OpenClaw npm target lookup helper, and update CLI regression tests with mocked package-manager calls.

## AI-assisted disclosure
This patch was prepared with AI assistance. I used source-level issue reproduction, targeted regression tests, and explicit command output to validate the behavior above.
